findings.title: readCostLog silently clears all records on corrupt JSONL line
findings.detail: src/utils/costs.ts:readCostLog wraps the entire read-and-parse operation in a single try/catch. If any line in .omc/costs.json is malformed (disk error, interrupted write, manual edit), JSON.parse throws and the outer catch returns [] — discarding all records. Budget enforcement then sees $0 spent for the sprint and cannot cap correctly, allowing unbounded token spend.
findings.fix_sketch: Move JSON.parse inside a per-line try/catch inside the .map(), skipping (and logging) unparseable lines instead of losing all records. Example: .flatMap(line => { try { return [JSON.parse(line)]; } catch { return []; } })

findings.title: Closes #0 appended to PR body for Discord-sourced tasks
findings.detail: src/pipeline/pr.ts:69 unconditionally appends `Closes #${input.task.issueNumber}` to every PR body. For Discord-sourced tasks, issueNumber is 0 (set in pipeline-bridge.ts:unifiedToPipelineTask), producing a spurious `Closes #0` footer. Every other callsite in the pipeline guards with `issueNumber > 0` (architect.ts:158, runner.ts:192, factory.ts:87) — only pr.ts is missing the guard.

findings.title: Docker sandbox runs as root with bridge network — insufficient isolation
findings.detail: src/agents/verifier/sandbox.ts buildDockerArgs() passes --user root and --network bridge to every docker run invocation. The file header comment says "no host network by default" but bridge gives full outbound internet access. Running as root means a container escape would have host root privileges. The pnpm store at /root/.pnpm-store is the reason root is used, but a non-root user with a custom store-dir would be safer.
findings.fix_sketch: Use --user 1000:1000 and --pnpm-store-dir /home/user/.pnpm-store (adding --user to the docker image's build). For typecheck/lint/test phases that don't need internet, use --network none. Update the header comment to accurately describe the current network policy.

findings.title: setupWorktree force-removes worktree by issueNumber — collision risk on retry race
findings.detail: src/pipeline/factory.ts:setupWorktree uses issueNumber as the worktree directory key for GitHub tasks. If a task is retried while a previous run is still in-flight (e.g., via /force-pr or a queue race), setupWorktree calls `git worktree remove --force` on the existing path, killing the first run's working directory mid-execution. Discord tasks are safe (they use task.id as key) but GitHub tasks are not.
findings.fix_sketch: Append the task ID to the key: `${issueNumber}-${task.id.slice(-6)}` so concurrent runs for the same issue never share a path. Update branchName derivation similarly.

findings.title: spawnCapture stdout/stderr accumulation has no size cap
findings.detail: src/repos/manager.ts:spawnCapture appends all git subprocess output to unbounded strings. For verbose or large git operations the output could grow arbitrarily. The verify/spawn-util.ts sibling correctly caps output at OUTPUT_BUFFER_CAP_BYTES; manager.ts has no analogous guard.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a byte cap (e.g. 2 MB) and truncated flag mirroring spawn-util.ts. Append a truncation notice to the error message when the cap is hit.

findings.title: Module-level readFileSync for routing.json has no error handling
findings.detail: src/classifier/index.ts calls readFileSync(resolve(REPO_ROOT, "config/routing.json")) at module load time without a try/catch. If the file is missing, the process crashes on the first import with a raw ENOENT stack trace that gives no hint about the config context.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap in try/catch and rethrow: throw new Error(`classifier: cannot load routing.json at ${path}: ${err.message}`)

findings.title: All editor commits use generic message "chore: editor changes"
findings.detail: src/pipeline/editor.ts hardcodes `git commit -m "chore: editor changes"` for every commit the editor stage produces. This makes the git log uninformative across all tasks and defeats conventional-commit auditability for the autonomously-generated diffs.
findings.fix_sketch: Use a descriptive message derived from the task title or the changed file list.

findings.title: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY passed to every Claude child process — exfiltration vector via prompt injection
findings.detail: The CLAUDE_ENV_ALLOWLIST in src/workers/claude-env.ts includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Editor/reviewer/doctor roles receive unsanitized plan content from Architect's LLM output, creating a real prompt-injection exfiltration path.
findings.fix_sketch: Restrict ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the anthropic-api adapter path only.

findings.title: Sprint tick() calls pickWorker() once per pending task in loop — redundant calls before acquire state propagates
findings.detail: When N pending tasks exist but 0 workers are available, pickWorker() was called N times inside the loop. Pre-loop check added: call pickWorker() once, skip loop entirely if null, re-check after each successful dispatch.
findings.fix_sketch: Declare nextWorkerId before the loop; break early if null; reassign after each dispatch.

findings.title: Untracked TODO for audit finding stuck in verifying state — no observable signal when doctor cap is exhausted
findings.detail: In pipeline/runner.ts, when the doctor retry cap was hit, the finding remained in verifying state indefinitely. Added maybeReopenAuditFinding() that resets verifying → reopened on cap exhaustion, both in .audits/index.json and Supabase.
findings.fix_sketch: Add maybeReopenAuditFinding() and call it before return failed() on doctor cap path.

findings.title: recoverStale() reads IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS without a lower-bound guard
findings.detail: If IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS is set to 0 (or a negative value), Number() returns 0 and the WHERE clause matches every in_flight task, immediately marking all of them failed without any retry.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the parsed value: const maxAttempts = Math.max(1, Number(process.env["IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS"] ?? 5));

findings.title: scheduleMorningDrain() callback can reschedule itself after stop() clears the timer
findings.detail: If stop() fires while the setTimeout callback is already executing, clearTimeout() is a no-op for the already-firing callback. The callback then calls this.scheduleMorningDrain() unconditionally, installing a new timer that is never cleared.
findings.fix_sketch: Add `if (!this.started) return;` as the first line of the scheduleMorningDrain() callback.

findings.title: planPhases() always schedules install even when no package.json exists in the worktree
findings.detail: readPackageJson() silently returns { scripts:{} } when package.json is absent, but planPhases() unconditionally set install to { skip: false }. For repos without package.json the Docker container would run pnpm install, fail, and report a spurious install failure.
findings.fix_sketch: Return a hasPackageJson flag from readPackageJson(); in planPhases() check that flag and set install to { skip: true } when false.

findings.title: Shutdown error catch blocks use console.warn instead of console.error
findings.detail: In daemon.ts the shutdown signal handler catches errors from orchestrator.stop(), client.destroy(), cp.stop(), and store.close() but logs them with console.warn. Convention expects errors in catch blocks to use console.error.
findings.fix_sketch: Change console.warn to console.error inside catch blocks that handle actual errors.

findings.title: MemoryNonceLedger sweep interval not guaranteed to clear in ControlPlane.stop()
findings.detail: Previously reported as open. On inspection of the current codebase, stop() already calls nonceStore.destroy() before server.close() — the fix was applied between the 2026-06-10 audit and this scan. Closing.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed: stop() calls nonceStore.destroy() before server.close().

findings.title: readFileSync for SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH in getKgPool() throws raw ENOENT if cert is missing
findings.detail: Introduced in the nightly-2026-06-10 SSL pinning change: readFileSync(process.env['SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH']) was called inline inside the PoolConfig literal without a try/catch. If the file is missing or unreadable, getKgPool() throws a raw Node.js ENOENT with a full file path in the message, leaking the cert path and giving no diagnostic context. Fixed by wrapping in loadCaCert() that throws KgPostgresUnavailableError.
findings.fix_sketch: Add loadCaCert(path) that wraps readFileSync in try/catch and throws KgPostgresUnavailableError.
